headline: BONANZA GRADE: West Red Lake Gold Drills 215 g/t Au Over 5.35m in Untapped Red Lake Zone
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines reported 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m — plus 50.34 g/t Au over 4.05m and 61.70 g/t Au over 3m — from the Austin 904 Complex at its Madsen Mine in Ontario's Red Lake Gold District, a previously unmined section of the deposit. Multiple high-grade holes confirm the 904 zone as a priority expansion target with initial production targeted for H1 2027.

headline: $2.4 BILLION COAL PLAY: Yancoal Seizes 80% of Queensland's Kestrel Coking Mine in Landmark ASX Deal
snippet: Yancoal Australia agreed on April 14 to pay up to $2.4 billion for an 80% stake in the Kestrel premium coking coal mine in Queensland's Bowen Basin, one of the largest Australian mining deals of 2026. Kestrel produced 5.9 million tonnes in 2025 and has approximately 25 years of mine life remaining; the deal is subject to FIRB and ACCC approvals and includes up to $550M in coal-price-linked contingent payments.

headline: NICKEL BENCHMARK SHOCK: Indonesia Hikes Ore Price More Than 2x, Sending Processors Scrambling
snippet: Indonesia's Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources issued a revised HPM (Harga Patokan Mineral) formula for nickel ore effective April 15, pushing the effective price for 1.5% grade ore from $26.66 to approximately $72.47 per wet metric tonne — a roughly 170% increase that directly hits downstream processors. Nickel futures surged on April 14 as traders priced in higher input costs for the world's largest nickel-producing nation.

headline: IBERIAN GOLD RUSH: Denarius Metals Proposes All-Share Takeover of Emerita Resources in Spain's Pyrite Belt
snippet: Denarius Metals delivered an unsolicited all-share proposal to acquire Emerita Resources Corp. on April 13, at a 15% premium to Emerita's April 10 close, aiming to consolidate projects across the Iberian Pyrite Belt in Spain. The combination would merge Denarius's Aguablanca processing facility with Emerita's Iberian Belt West deposit, potentially eliminating standalone capex for Emerita's concentrate plant.

headline: AU-US CRITICAL MINERALS PACT: Both Nations Pledge $1B+ Each as Rare Earth Supply Chain Partnership Formalises
snippet: Australia's Minister for Resources released a communiqué on April 13 confirming expanded bilateral cooperation with the United States on critical minerals and rare earth supply chains, with each nation committing a minimum of $1 billion in financing to key miners under the US-Australia Critical Minerals Framework. The agreement targets supply chain diversification away from Chinese-controlled processing for materials critical to defence systems, EV batteries, and grid-scale energy storage.

headline: KAMOA-KAKULA DELIVERS: Ivanhoe Mines Posts 71,417t Copper in Q1 2026 as Recovery Plan Advances
snippet: Ivanhoe Mines reported 71,417 tonnes of copper in blister and anode from the Kamoa-Kakula complex in DRC in Q1 2026, comprised of 63,671 tonnes from its on-site direct-to-blister smelter and 7,746 tonnes from the Lualaba Copper Smelter, as ongoing infrastructure recovery efforts advance. The result confirms Kamoa-Kakula's position as one of the highest-grade and highest-volume copper mines in production globally.

headline: THE NEW GILDED AGE: Newmont and Barrick Hit 70% Margins as Gold Above $4,500 Rewrites Mining Economics
snippet: An April 14 analysis argues that gold sustained above $4,500/oz has pushed top-tier miners into a fundamentally different profit regime — nearly $3,000 per ounce of margin versus the 20-30% they scraped for a decade — with Newmont crossing $120/share and Barrick doubling in 18 months. The piece also details the shadow hanging over the sector: the escalating Newmont-Barrick dispute over Nevada Gold Mines, where a February Notice of Default and allegations of resource diversion could become a material risk.

headline: LITHIUM NEARLY DOUBLES: Battery-Grade Prices Hit $26,000/t After China VAT Shift and Zimbabwe Export Ban
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate surged to approximately $26,278 per tonne through Q1 2026 — nearly double its late-2025 lows — driven by China's phased elimination of VAT rebates on battery exports (effective April 1) and Zimbabwe's surprise February suspension of all raw lithium concentrate exports. Spodumene crossed $2,000/t for the first time since late 2023, and Core Lithium's Finniss project has received a green light to restart with first ore expected in May, signalling a structural floor in the market.

headline: ATHABASCA URANIUM RACE: Vanguard Mining Launches Phase 1 Exploration at Nucleon Project Near NexGen's Arrow
snippet: Vanguard Mining Corp. announced on April 14 the start of Phase 1 exploration at its Nucleon Uranium Project in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin — a 23,424-hectare land position directly adjacent to the regional setting that hosts Fission Uranium's Patterson Lake South and NexGen Energy's Arrow deposits. The initial program includes systematic lake-sediment sampling and multi-element geochemistry targeting uranium and pathfinder elements across all seven claim blocks.

headline: RESERVE CRISIS AHEAD: Gold and Silver Miners Are Cashing Out Instead of Exploring — And It Will Cost Them
snippet: A Sprott Money analysis argues that a decade of exploration underinvestment has left major gold and silver producers dangerously thin on reserve replacement, meaning today's super-margins may mask a structural production cliff arriving in the late 2020s. The piece contends that the companies most likely to outperform longer-term are not the buyback-heavy seniors but the well-capitalised juniors still actively building resource bases.
